title: Post Workout Protein Shake
metaDescription: Post workout protein shake with 32g of protein and fast-digesting carbs — banana, Greek yogurt, and peanut butter blended in under 5 minutes.
author: Recipe Team
keywords: post workout protein shake, post workout meal, protein shake recipe
ingredients: 1 cup milk (dairy or unsweetened almond milk)

steps.name: Blend everything
steps.text: Add milk, Greek yogurt, banana, peanut butter, honey, and ice to a blender.

steps.name: Blend until smooth
steps.text: Blend on high for 30-45 seconds until completely smooth and frothy.

steps.name: Drink promptly
steps.text: Pour into a glass and drink within 30-45 minutes of finishing your workout for the best benefit.

tips.question: Why drink this shortly after a workout instead of anytime?
tips.answer: Many athletes aim to get protein and carbs in within an hour or so post-workout, when the body is primed to use them for muscle repair and glycogen replenishment — waiting several hours is generally considered less ideal, though total daily intake still matters most.

tips.question: How much protein should a post-workout shake have?
tips.answer: A commonly cited range is 20-40g of protein per serving, depending on body size and training intensity — this shake lands at 32g, in the middle of that range.

tips.question: Can I make this dairy-free?
tips.answer: Yes — swap the milk for unsweetened almond or oat milk and the Greek yogurt for a dairy-free yogurt alternative, though the protein content will drop somewhat.

tips.question: Why include a banana instead of just protein?
tips.answer: The carbs from the banana and honey help replenish glycogen (the energy your muscles burned during training) — a shake with protein alone skips that part of recovery.

storyIntro: The window right after a workout is when a lot of people reach for a shaker bottle of powder and water — this is the same idea, just built from a banana, Greek yogurt, and peanut butter instead of a scoop.
It blends in under five minutes, lands at 32g of protein, and the banana and honey add the fast carbs your muscles are looking to restock after training.
storyNote: Drink it within about 30-45 minutes of finishing your workout if you can — that's the window most commonly cited for getting the most benefit from post-workout nutrition, though don't stress if it's not exact.
